title: lepeocercis mollicoma
intro: Lepeocercis mollicoma, also known as the hairy wood sorrel, is a plant species that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family, typically found in tropical regions. The medicinal properties of Lepeocercis mollicoma lie in its high content of oxalic acid and flavonoids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of consuming the plant or its herbal preparations include the potential treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and skin conditions such as eczema. Lepeocercis mollicoma is often used in traditional medicine and can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction for its medicinal properties.
